benefits.title: Students curate and communicate credible accounts of their learning
benefits.statement: Students and graduates use the enhanced transcript to select, organise and connect the knowledge, capabilities and experiences developed through their course and broader King’s experience. They curate audience-relevant narratives about what they know, can do and contribute, drawing appropriately on gains that are certified, evidenced or claimed. This makes credible articulation less dependent on confidence, networks or familiarity with professional language.
benefits.successLooksLike: Students actively use the transcript during study and at key transition points to curate and externalise narratives about the value they take into the world. These narratives select and connect relevant learning gains, explain why they matter, distinguish between certified, evidenced and claimed capabilities, and point to appropriate evidence.

benefits.title: King’s can demonstrate the distinctive capabilities and relevance of its education
benefits.statement: King’s uses responsibly aggregated transcript profiles to demonstrate the knowledge and capabilities developed through its education, retaining the distinctive contribution of individual courses within the institutional picture. This provides a stronger evidence base for engaging government, business, industry and wider society on what King’s graduates know, can do and contribute.
benefits.beneficiary: King’s, students, graduates and external partners
benefits.successLooksLike: King’s routinely uses governed aggregate profiles in external engagement and institutional narratives. These accounts show both the distinctiveness of individual courses and patterns across King’s, and are grounded in the underlying transcript evidence.
